hero_tag: Educational Course · Bilingual · AI & Māori Data Sovereignty
hero_sub: Six modules exploring AI, Māori data sovereignty, tikanga, and Indigenous control over digital infrastructure — taught bilingually in te reo Māori and English.
social_kit_intro: 30 motifs across 11 cultures. Each with activist meme images, motif artwork, hashtag sets, and course module links. CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 — free to use and share.
kaitiaki_banner_text: Te Pā Tūwatawata is moving from website to registered charitable trust. We are inviting expressions of interest for language-kaitiaki seats — one seat per language community, appointed by that community.
kaitiaki_intro: Te Pā Tūwatawata is being established as a registered charitable trust in Aotearoa New Zealand. The trust deed reserves a kaitiaki seat for each language community hosted on this site. As te reo Māori, Gagana Sāmoa, Avañe’ẽ (Guaraní), Português, العربية and others are added, a seat is held open for the community whose language is being carried — to be filled by that community, on their terms.
kaitiaki_principle_1: Māori-led governance. The deed requires majority Māori trusteeship and a Māori chair. Te Tiriti o Waitangi is the foundation, not an afterthought.
kaitiaki_principle_2: One seat per language community. A kaitiaki seat opens only when a language goes live on the site — and is appointed by that community, not by the existing board.
kaitiaki_principle_3: Free, prior and informed consent. No language is added without a community partner asking for it. This follows the Maiam nayri Wingara, CARE and OCAP principles already governing this site.
kaitiaki_principle_4: Not open recruitment. Self-nomination to a kaitiaki seat is not how these seats are filled. We are inviting expressions of interest so communities can identify and put forward their own kaitiaki.
kaitiaki_seat_mi_note: Multiple Māori seats including the chair. Appointment by hapū / iwi partners, with rangatiratanga of the kaupapa held by tangata whenua.
kaitiaki_seat_sm_note: Seat opens as Gagana Sāmoa goes live on the site. Appointment by a Samoan community partner.
kaitiaki_seat_gn_note: Seat opens as Avañe’ẽ goes live. Appointment by a Guaraní community partner in consultation with the Te Pā board.
kaitiaki_seat_more_note: A seat is held open for any language community whose reo is added to the site — Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander, Pasifika, Amazonian and others, only on community request.
kaitiaki_eoi_heading: Ko wai mā te kaitiaki o tō reo?
kaitiaki_eoi_body: If your language community would like to take up a kaitiaki seat — or recommend the person who should hold it — please get in touch. We are not asking individuals to self-nominate. We are asking communities to put forward their own kaitiaki, on their own terms, at their own pace.
kaitiaki_eoi_footnote: Te Pā Tūwatawata is being registered as a charitable trust under the Charitable Trusts Act 1957 and will apply for registration with Charities Services. Trustees become officers under the Charities Act 2005 and must be 18+ and not disqualified.
